plan_metadata._status_values: draft | pending-approval | queued | in-progress | done | failed
plan_metadata._comment: task_id and task_name must match the originating request file: N-short-descriptive-name.md

dependencies._comment: required_tasks lists task IDs that must be 'done' before this plan can execute. packages lists any new dependencies to install. affected_modules lists project modules this plan touches.

stages.name: Stage Name
stages.description: What this stage accomplishes and why it exists as a discrete unit of work.
stages.rollback_plan: Specific steps to revert this stage if it fails.
stages._complexity_values: small | medium | large — if 'large', consider splitting into smaller stages

stages._comment: Every plan MUST end with these two stages (renumber as needed). The content of each stage is plan-specific — the planning agent decides which spec files, docs, and README sections need updating based on what the plan changes.

stages.name: Spec Updates
stages.description: MANDATORY — Update agent-development/agent-specs/ files to reflect any architectural, structural, or convention changes introduced by this plan.
stages.rollback_plan: Revert spec files to their pre-plan state via git checkout.

stages.name: Documentation Updates
stages.description: MANDATORY — Update human-facing documentation (README.md, DEVELOPMENT-GUIDE.md, and any other relevant docs) to reflect changes introduced by this plan.
stages.rollback_plan: Revert documentation files to their pre-plan state via git checkout.
